processAlternatives: Automated taping heads for the surviving full-coverage runs

title: Zonal e-fuse power distribution enables harness downsizing
description: [ALL] Melting fuses force every protected circuit to be wired for the fuse's worst-case clearing energy rather than the load's actual demand, and the central fuse boxes force star topology with long individual runs. Replacing the front and rear junction…

ideaData.title: Zonal e-fuse power distribution enables harness downsizing
ideaData.technicalDescription: Melting fuses force every protected circuit to be wired for the fuse's worst-case clearing energy rather than the load's actual demand, and the central fuse boxes force star topology with long individual runs. Replacing the front and rear junction boxes with two zonal controllers using MOSFET high-side e-fuse channels changes the sizing rule: wire cross-section is chosen for load current and voltage drop, with the e-fuse's microsecond trip protecting even downsized conductors, and loads pick up power from the nearest zone rather than a central box. Tesla's Model 3 onward and Rivian's R1T zonal architectures are the production evidence that this deletes kilograms of copper and dozens of harness branches; applied to this programme's body domain only, conservative resizing recovers about 1.9 kg of copper, deletes 23 fuse positions, five relays and two fuse-box part numbers, and shortens total circuit length around 14 percent. The e-fuse silicon and zonal ECUs add material cost back, which is why the honest net lands at the modest end for a combustion-era platform rather than the headline figures quoted for clean-sheet EVs. Diagnostic capability improves as a side effect, each channel reporting current and fault state over CAN, retiring the unfused-short warranty black hole. Software-controlled load shedding also trims the alternator sizing case, an adjacent saving booked separately.
ideaData.materialGrade: AEC-Q101 MOSFET e-fuse stages / FLRY-B resized per load
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Two fuse boxes, 23 fuses, five relays and their bus bars leave the BOM; two zonal controllers enter it. Harness branch count falls double-digit percent, simplifying board layouts, and end-of-line electrical test gains per-channel diagnostics instead of fuse-pull checks.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Wire sized for load, not fuse physics, across the body power domain.

ideaData.riskNotes: E-fuse trip curves must be characterised against every load's inrush signature or nuisance trips plague launch; motor and lamp loads are the known offenders. Functional-safety classification of channels feeding lighting and wipers imposes ISO 26262 development rigour on the zonal controller, the cost item that erodes weak business cases. Downsized conductors depend permanently on the e-fuse being present, so service substitution with standard fuses must be made physically impossible.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Tesla Model 3 / Rivian R1T zonal power distribution
ideaData.regulatoryContext: ISO 26262 for safety-relevant channels; LV124 electrical robustness for controllers
materialAlternatives: Hybrid box retaining melting fuses on highest-energy circuits
processAlternatives: Body-domain-only rollout vs full-vehicle zonal
Smart-fuse retrofit modules into existing box footprint

title: Primerless 3-wet compact paint process deletes primer bake
description: [ALL] Paint chemistry has quietly outrun this plant's process layout: the line still runs the classic four-coat sequence, e-coat bake, primer spray and full 160 degree Celsius bake, then basecoat and clearcoat with a final bake, although modern waterborne…

ideaData.title: Primerless 3-wet compact paint process deletes primer bake
ideaData.technicalDescription: Paint chemistry has quietly outrun this plant's process layout: the line still runs the classic four-coat sequence, e-coat bake, primer spray and full 160 degree Celsius bake, then basecoat and clearcoat with a final bake, although modern waterborne systems no longer require the intermediate oven pass. Converting to a 3-wet compact process applies a functionalised waterborne primer-surfacer, waterborne basecoat and 2K clearcoat wet-on-wet-on-wet with only a short 80 degree Celsius flash between layers and a single final bake. Mazda's Aqua-tech system and Toyota's equivalent have run this in volume production for over a decade, with BASF, PPG and Axalta all offering qualified 3-wet chemistries, so the materials risk is genuinely retired; the engineering effort is plant conversion and colour requalification. Deleting the primer oven pass removes roughly 25 percent of paint-shop gas consumption per body plus the oven's maintenance and floor-time, shortens process time by around 40 minutes per body, and releases the primer bake zone as future capacity. Film performance, stone-chip per ISO 20567-1, humidity resistance, and appearance measured as DOI and orange peel by wavescan, must requalify per colour, which is why the rollout plan sequences high-volume colours first and holds low-runners on the legacy process during transition.
ideaData.materialGrade: Waterborne primer-surfacer and basecoat / 2K polyurethane clearcoat
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Primer bake oven and its gas, filtration and maintenance load exit the cost-per-unit; process time falls ~40 minutes per body. Booth zones re-balance for wet-on-wet flash-off, and colour requalification consumes lab and line-trial capacity for several quarters.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One full oven pass removed from every body built.

ideaData.riskNotes: Appearance harmony across body and plastic parts painted off-line is the launch risk, since the 3-wet film stack meters colour differently; bumper-to-fender match needs per-colour master panels signed off before customer builds. Stone-chip performance at reduced total film build must pass ISO 20567-1 on the vulnerable rocker and leading-edge zones. VOC compliance improves with waterborne layers but the 2K clearcoat isocyanate handling and REACH diisocyanate training obligations remain and transition-period dual running doubles chemistry inventory temporarily.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Mazda Aqua-tech 3-wet production system

evidenceSources.title: 3-wet adoption at Mazda, Toyota and followers

title: Zirconium thin-film pretreatment replaces trication zinc phosphate
description: [ALL] Body pretreatment runs a trication zinc phosphate line: multi-stage conditioning, a phosphate bath held at 50 to 55 degrees Celsius generating continuous sludge that is landfilled as filter cake, nickel and manganese in the chemistry, and…

ideaData.title: Zirconium thin-film pretreatment replaces trication zinc phosphate
ideaData.technicalDescription: Body pretreatment runs a trication zinc phosphate line: multi-stage conditioning, a phosphate bath held at 50 to 55 degrees Celsius generating continuous sludge that is landfilled as filter cake, nickel and manganese in the chemistry, and post-rinses to match. Thin-film zirconium oxide conversion coating, Henkel's Bonderite M-NT family or Chemetall's Oxsilan, replaces the phosphate stage with a bath running at ambient to 30 degrees Celsius, cutting bath heating energy by the temperature delta, reducing sludge generation by more than 90 percent, and removing nickel from the house chemistry, a REACH exposure and wastewater burden that carries real compliance cost. The zirconium film deposits at 20 to 100 nanometres versus 2 to 3 microns of phosphate crystal, so coating-weight chemical consumption falls by an order of magnitude per body. Multiple North American and European OEM plants converted over the past decade, making this a fast-follower action rather than pioneering. Corrosion validation is the substantive engineering: cyclic corrosion per each OEM's scab and stone-chip-plus-salt protocols across the full substrate mix, since mixed-metal bodies with high aluminium content actually favour zirconium chemistry, while hot-dip galvanised patches need the additive package tuned. E-coat adhesion transfers cleanly in supplier data but is re-proven on this body's substrate set before cutover.
ideaData.materialGrade: Zirconium oxide conversion film 20–100 nm (Bonderite M-NT class)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Bath heating load drops with the ~25 degree Celsius operating delta, sludge disposal falls >90%, and two rinse stages simplify. Existing tanks convert with nozzle and dosing changes rather than new civils, keeping conversion inside a summer shutdown window.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Chemical mass, bath heat and sludge disposal all drop an order class.

ideaData.riskNotes: Corrosion performance on hot-dip galvanised steel is the historical weak point of thin-film chemistry and must pass the OEM cyclic protocol on this body's worst-case seam and hem-flange coupons before any cutover date is set. Line-speed sensitivity is higher than phosphate, so bath monitoring moves from shift sampling to continuous conductivity and zirconium-concentration control. A validated re-phosphate fallback plan protects launch if scab results regress at plant scale.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Henkel Bonderite M-NT conversions at NA/EU OEM plants

evidenceSources.title: Thin-film pretreatment plant conversion case studies

ideaData.regulatoryContext: REACH nickel restrictions; wastewater discharge permits; EU IED best-available-technique alignment

title: E-coat film build reduction through throwpower optimisation
description: [ALL] Measured e-coat film builds across audit bodies tell the usual story: 26 to 30 microns on exterior skins against a 20-micron functional requirement, because voltage and bath solids are set high enough to drag marginal interior cavities up to their…

ideaData.title: E-coat film build reduction through throwpower optimisation
ideaData.technicalDescription: Measured e-coat film builds across audit bodies tell the usual story: 26 to 30 microns on exterior skins against a 20-micron functional requirement, because voltage and bath solids are set high enough to drag marginal interior cavities up to their 12-micron minimum, over-coating everything the current reaches easily. The optimisation attacks the spread rather than the average. High-throwpower e-coat chemistry, BASF CathoGuard 800-class or PPG equivalent, deposits more film in recessed cavities per volt, and is paired with anode reconfiguration, adding two auxiliary anodes at the sill and B-pillar cavities identified as the limiting interiors, plus a revised voltage ramp profile in the first 60 seconds of immersion. With cavities no longer limiting, average exterior build settles at 21 to 22 microns, cutting paste and resin consumption 18 to 20 percent per body, a figure consistent with published conversions at European plants. Corrosion capability is preserved because interior minima are maintained or improved; exterior skins never needed 28 microns, they got them as a by-product. Validation is film-build mapping on 40 drilled-coupon points across three body styles, plus standard cyclic corrosion on the previously marginal cavities. Bath management tightens, with solids, pH and conductivity control windows narrowed to hold the leaner operating point stable across seasonal temperature swings.
ideaData.materialGrade: High-throwpower cathodic epoxy e-coat (CathoGuard 800 class)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Paste and resin purchase drops ~18% per body with no cycle-time change; two auxiliary anodes and a ramp-profile update are the only hardware items. Film-build mapping becomes a quarterly audit routine to hold the leaner setpoint.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Over-coated exterior skins brought down to requirement, cavities protected by chemistry.

ideaData.riskNotes: Thinner average film narrows the margin for substrate roughness telegraphing through to the primer layer, so surface-finish interaction is checked on the roughest steel supplier's coils specifically. Any future body-style addition with deeper cavities re-opens the anode configuration. Warranty exposure is protected by keeping interior minima unchanged and by holding a two-micron guard band above the corrosion-qualified minimum on exteriors rather than running to the edge.
dfmaPrinciples: Process capability investment over material spend
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BASF CathoGuard high-throwpower conversions, European plants
ideaData.regulatoryContext: Corrosion warranty commitments; wastewater permit unchanged; EU IED alignment
materialAlternatives: PPG Enviro-Prime equivalent chemistry

processAlternatives: Membrane anode refurbishment concurrent with conversion

title: High-transfer-efficiency clearcoat application with dry separation
description: [ALL] Clearcoat is the most expensive liquid in the shop per litre and the current booth wastes a third of it: air-assisted electrostatic application achieves about 65 percent transfer efficiency, with overspray captured in a water-wash venturi whose…

ideaData.title: High-transfer-efficiency clearcoat application with dry separation
ideaData.technicalDescription: Clearcoat is the most expensive liquid in the shop per litre and the current booth wastes a third of it: air-assisted electrostatic application achieves about 65 percent transfer efficiency, with overspray captured in a water-wash venturi whose sludge chemistry, water treatment and conditioned-air load are all paid for by the paint that missed the car. The upgrade combines three proven Dürr elements: EcoBell3 high-rotation bell-bell application on both clearcoat robots lifts transfer efficiency toward 80 percent for the 2K polyurethane clearcoat at 58 percent solids; dry limestone-powder separation (EcoDryScrubber class) replaces the water wash, allowing up to 85 percent recirculated booth air and deleting the sludge water-treatment chain for this booth; and closed-loop film-thickness feedback from inline measurement trims fluid delivery per style. Material consumption per body falls with the transfer-efficiency gain, roughly 18 percent of clearcoat purchase, while booth energy drops with air recirculation since conditioning outside air dominates booth operating cost. VOC mass emitted falls proportionally with sprayed volume, easing EU IED solvent-management-plan headroom without abatement investment. Multiple German and Chinese plants run this exact configuration in series production. Appearance must be requalified because bell-bell atomisation shifts droplet spectra, checked by wavescan orange-peel targets on horizontal and vertical panels per colour family.
ideaData.materialGrade: 2K PU clearcoat, 58% solids / limestone capture powder
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Clearcoat purchase falls ~18% and the booth's water-treatment chain is deleted, with limestone powder recycled into the cement industry offtake. Bell changeout and inline thickness gauging integrate at two robot stations during a planned shutdown.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Paint that used to coat the scrubber water now coats cars.

ideaData.riskNotes: Bell-bell clearcoat on vertical surfaces risks sag at the higher delivery rates needed for takt, so the robot path and fluid map DOE must precede any commitment on cycle time. Dry-scrubber powder handling introduces a combustible-dust ATEX assessment absent from the wet booth. Appearance harmony with off-line painted plastic parts is re-checked because droplet-spectrum changes shift DOI slightly even at matched film build; REACH diisocyanate handling obligations carry over unchanged.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Dürr EcoBell3 with EcoDryScrubber installations, German and Chinese plants
ideaData.regulatoryContext: EU IED VOC solvent management plan; ATEX dust assessment; REACH diisocyanates
materialAlternatives: 1K silane-modified clearcoat where OEM approves durability set
